req.title: Create medication request (negative)
req.statement: A prescribing clinician shall issue a MedicationRequest; requests for controlled substances shall require schedule verification. Negative paths including permission denial, missing mandatory elements, and conflicting state transitions must be tested.

doc.reasoning: The violation was in test[4]: a negative kind test was incorrectly tagged with heuristic=STC. STC (State Transition Coverage) is only appropriate when the test exercises valid state machine paths; negative state-transition tests (e.g., attempting an illegal transition from 'cancelled' back to 'active') must use heuristic=NEG because the primary intent is error-path validation, not coverage of a valid state graph arc. The fix changes test[4] heuristic from STC to NEG while preserving all other tests and their content intact. Tests 0-3 are unchanged from the passing original.

tests.name: test_create_medication_request_valid_prescriber_non_controlled
actions: Build a well-formed MedicationRequest payload for a non-controlled substance
Capture HTTP status code and response body
trace: R-HLT-058: prescribing clinician shall issue a MedicationRequest

tests.name: test_create_cii_medication_request_without_schedule_verification_returns_403
preconditions: Prescriber account does NOT have schedule-II verification flag set
actions: Build a MedicationRequest payload for a Schedule II controlled substance (e.g., oxycodone)
Capture HTTP status code and response body
expected: Response body contains error code SCHEDULE_VERIFICATION_REQUIRED or equivalent
trace: R-HLT-058: requests for controlled substances shall require schedule verification
R-HLT-058: Error pathway – 403 without schedule verification for CII
tests.executable:
import requests

BASE_URL = "http://localhost:8080/fhir"

def test_create_cii_medication_request_without_schedule_verification_returns_403(
        prescriber_token_no_schedule, patient_id):
    payload = {
        "resourceType": "MedicationRequest",
        "status": "active",
        "intent": "order",
        "subject": {"reference": f"Patient/{patient_id}"},
        "medicationCodeableConcept": {
            "coding": [{"system": "http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm",
                         "code": "1049611", "display": "Oxycodone 5mg"}],
            "extension": [{"url": "http://fhir-lite/controlled-schedule",
                            "valueCode": "CII"}]
        },
        "requester": {"reference": "Practitioner/prescriber-001"}
    }
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {prescriber_token_no_schedule}",
               "Content-Type": "application/fhir+json"}
    resp = requests.post(f"{BASE_URL}/MedicationRequest", json=payload, headers=headers)
    assert resp.status_code == 403
    body = resp.json()
    issue_codes = [i.get("details", {}).get("text", "") for i in body.get("issue", [])]
    assert any("SCHEDULE_VERIFICATION" in c.upper() for c in issue_codes)

tests.name: test_create_medication_request_exactly_minimum_mandatory_fields
preconditions: Request payload contains ONLY the mandatory FHIR MedicationRequest fields (status, intent, subject, medication)
actions: Build a MedicationRequest payload with exactly the four mandatory FHIR R4 fields and nothing more
Capture HTTP status code and response body
expected: Response body id field is populated by server
trace: R-HLT-058: missing mandatory elements must be tested (boundary at minimum valid set)

tests.name: test_create_medication_request_multiple_missing_mandatory_fields
preconditions: Request payload is missing both 'intent' and 'medicationCodeableConcept'
actions: Build a MedicationRequest payload omitting 'intent' and 'medicationCodeableConcept'
Capture HTTP status code and each OperationOutcome issue
expected: Response body contains OperationOutcome with at least two distinct issue entries
trace: R-HLT-058: missing mandatory elements must be tested
R-HLT-058: Error pathways – multiple simultaneous missing fields (DT rule)
tests.executable:
import requests

BASE_URL = "http://localhost:8080/fhir"

def test_create_medication_request_multiple_missing_mandatory_fields(prescriber_token, patient_id):
    # Decision table: intent=MISSING, medication=MISSING, status=present, subject=present
    payload = {
        "resourceType": "MedicationRequest",
        "status": "active",
        "subject": {"reference": f"Patient/{patient_id}"}
        # 'intent' and 'medicationCodeableConcept' deliberately omitted
    }
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {prescriber_token}",
               "Content-Type": "application/fhir+json"}
    resp = requests.post(f"{BASE_URL}/MedicationRequest", json=payload, headers=headers)
    assert resp.status_code == 422
    body = resp.json()
    assert body.get("resourceType") == "OperationOutcome"
    issues = body.get("issue", [])
    assert len(issues) >= 2
    diagnostics = " ".join(i.get("diagnostics", "") + i.get("expression", [""])[0] if i.get("expression") else i.get("diagnostics", "") for i in issues).lower()
    assert "intent" in diagnostics
    assert "medication" in diagnostics

tests.name: test_reactivate_cancelled_medication_request_returns_error
preconditions: A MedicationRequest resource exists in status 'cancelled' with id <CANCELLED_REQUEST_ID>
FHIR-lite service enforces terminal state for 'cancelled' status
actions: Build a PUT/PATCH payload attempting to transition the cancelled MedicationRequest to status 'active'
Submit the request to PUT /MedicationRequest/<CANCELLED_REQUEST_ID> with Authorization: Bearer <prescriber_token>
Capture HTTP status code and OperationOutcome response body
expected: HTTP 409 Conflict or HTTP 422 Unprocessable Entity
Response body contains OperationOutcome describing an illegal state transition
MedicationRequest remains in 'cancelled' status (GET confirms no status change)
trace: R-HLT-058: conflicting state transitions must be tested
R-HLT-058: negative path – illegal reactivation of a cancelled request
tests.executable:
import requests

BASE_URL = "http://localhost:8080/fhir"

def test_reactivate_cancelled_medication_request_returns_error(
        prescriber_token, cancelled_medication_request_id):
    # Attempt to illegally transition a cancelled request back to active
    update_payload = {
        "resourceType": "MedicationRequest",
        "id": cancelled_medication_request_id,
        "status": "active",
        "intent": "order",
        "subject": {"reference": "Patient/patient-001"},
        "medicationCodeableConcept": {
            "coding": [{"system": "http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm",
                         "code": "1049502", "display": "Amoxicillin 500mg"}]
        }
    }
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {prescriber_token}",
               "Content-Type": "application/fhir+json"}
    resp = requests.put(
        f"{BASE_URL}/MedicationRequest/{cancelled_medication_request_id}",
        json=update_payload,
        headers=headers
    )
    assert resp.status_code in (409, 422), (
        f"Expected 409 or 422 for illegal state transition, got {resp.status_code}"
    )
    body = resp.json()
    assert body.get("resourceType") == "OperationOutcome"
    # Confirm resource is still cancelled
    get_resp = requests.get(
        f"{BASE_URL}/MedicationRequest/{cancelled_medication_request_id}",
        headers=headers
    )
    assert get_resp.status_code == 200
    assert get_resp.json().get("status") == "cancelled"
